answer:
An undirected graph has an Euler circuit if and only if it is connected and every vertex has even degree.  
A complete graph \(K_n\) has degree \(n-1\) for each vertex, so it has an Euler circuit only when \(n-1\) is even, i.e., \(n\) is odd; thus \(K_{13}\) works but \(K_{12}\) does not.  
A tree with more than one vertex always has leaves (degree 1), so it cannot have an Euler circuit.  
Therefore, only II is correct.

\boxed{H}
